description: '''Math rock''' is a style of [[Alternative rock|alternative]] and [[indie rock]] with roots in bands such as [[King Crimson]] and [[Rush (band)|Rush]]. It is characterized by complex, atypical [[rhythm]]ic structures (including irregular stopping and starting), [[counterpoint]], odd [[time signatures]], and [[extended chord]]s. Math rock has been classified as a derivative or subgenre of [[experimental rock]], sharing its [[Avant-garde music|avant-garde]] ethos and emphasis on [[Through-composed music|through-composed structure]] rather than traditional song forms. The genre has additionally been described as bearing similarities to [[post-rock]], as the "opposite side of the same coin". Opting for a "[[rock music|rockier]]" approach to songwriting and [[timbres]], the style is often performed by [[music ensemble|smaller ensembles]] which emphasize the role of the [[guitar]].
